title_en: Chinese EV Batteries Are Eating the World
title_zh: 中国电动汽车电池正在席卷全球

paragraphs.en: Here are 23 ways China is rewiring the future.
paragraphs.zh: 中国正在以23种方式重塑未来。

paragraphs.en: THE symbolism was clear last June when Emmanuel Macron, surrounded by factory workers, held up a sleek lithium battery in his right hand and a mining lamp in his left. He was in Douai, a northern French city with a coal mining history dating back to the 1700s. The city is now also the site of a battery factory, which would allow France to produce all parts of electric vehicles domestically. This factory, Macron declared, represented an “economic and ecological revolution.”
paragraphs.zh: 这种象征意义在去年6月表现得淋漓尽致：当时埃马纽埃尔·马克龙站在工厂工人中间，右手举着一块线条流畅的锂电池，左手举着一盏矿灯。他身处法国北部城市杜艾，这座城市有着可追溯到18世纪的煤矿历史。如今，这里也成为一座电池工厂的所在地，这座工厂将使法国能够在国内生产电动汽车所需的所有零部件。马克龙称，这座工厂代表着一场“经济与生态革命”。

paragraphs.en: Macron immediately acknowledged that France didn’t pull this off alone: “We brought in investors from the other side of the world. They transferred their technologies. They helped train people,” Macron said, gesturing at a man beside him.
paragraphs.zh: 马克龙也立即承认，法国并不是单靠自己完成这一切的：“我们从世界另一端吸引来了投资者。他们转移了技术。也帮助我们培训人才，”马克龙说着，向身边的一位男士做出示意。

paragraphs.en: The man was Zhang Lei, the founder of Envision, a prominent Chinese company that makes wind turbines and lithium batteries. Its battery arm is investing up to €2 billion in this Douai factory and, more importantly, contributed the expertise for efficient mass production. He and Macron grabbed markers and signed their names on the first battery produced in Douai. “Thank you, Chairman, because you trusted us and because you did exactly what you said you would do,” Macron said, looking straight into Zhang’s eyes.
paragraphs.zh: 那位男士是张磊，远景能源的创始人。远景是一家著名的中国企业，生产风电机组和锂电池。该公司旗下电池业务正在这座杜艾工厂投资高达20亿欧元，更重要的是，它贡献了实现高效规模化生产所需的关键技术。马克龙和张磊一起拿起马克笔，在杜艾工厂生产的第一块电池上签下名字。“谢谢您，张主席，因为您信任我们，也因为您真的做到了您当初说的每一件事，”马克龙凝视着张磊的眼睛说道。

paragraphs.en: In 2026, it’s OK to nerd out at parties about batteries. Lithium batteries are turning solar and wind into 24/7 stable energy sources. Battery-powered cars are shaking up the multitrillion-dollar automotive industry and made Elon Musk the richest man on Earth. Lithium batteries even won a Nobel Prize, and the US government now categorizes lithium as a “critical mineral.”
paragraphs.zh: 在2026年，拿电池这件事在派对上聊上一阵已经很正常了。锂电池正在把太阳能和风能变成24小时稳定的能源。电池驱动的汽车正在重构价值数万亿美元的汽车行业，并让埃隆·马斯克成为地球上最富有的人。锂电池甚至赢得了诺贝尔奖，而美国政府现在也把锂列为“关键矿产”。

paragraphs.en: Lithium’s rising tides lifted one set of boats more than others—China’s battalion of battery companies. After decades of quiet growth, firms such as CATL, BYD, Gotion High-Tech, and Envision are now primary suppliers for the world’s EVs and energy grids. In 2024, more than 80 percent of the world’s battery cells were produced in China, according to the International Energy Agency. Now those companies are expanding beyond China’s borders. In the past decade they’ve built or announced at least 68 factories outside China, according to data collected by WIRED and the Rhodium Group, a New York–based think tank.
paragraphs.zh: 锂产业的上升浪把一拨船只推得更快，尤其是中国这一支电池企业大队。经过数十年低调扩张，像宁德时代、比亚迪、国轩高科和远景能源这样的公司，现已成为全球电动车和电网的主要供应商。国际能源署数据显示，2024年全球超过80%的电池单体是在中国生产的。如今，这些企业正在走出中国国界。根据纽约智库Rhodium Group与《连线》杂志整理的数据，在过去十年里，这些公司在海外已建成或宣布建设至少68座工厂。

paragraphs.en: Collectively, per the Rhodium Group, the factories represent an investment of more than $45 billion in the rest of the world. They also reflect a big shift in what manufacturing dominance looks like. “Made in China” used to be—and still often is—a label for cheap labor, knockoffs, and $5 gadgets. Now it also means state-of-the-art technology assembled anywhere in the world.
paragraphs.zh: 根据Rhodium Group，这些工厂在世界其他地区的总投资超过450亿美元。它们也反映了制造业“谁在主导世界”这一判断方式的巨大变化。“中国制造”过去常常是一个廉价劳动力、山寨品和5美元小产品的标签；如今，它也意味着可在世界任何地方组装的前沿技术。

paragraphs.en: “We believe it’s a new phase. We have never really seen that in Chinese overseas investments,” says Armand Meyer, a senior research analyst at Rhodium Group. According to his calculations, 2024 was the first year Chinese EV and battery companies spent more money building factories outside of China than within. “They are ready to leave the domestic market, and they are as competitive as traditional Western players, or even more competitive,” Meyer continues. “We think it’s just the beginning.”
paragraphs.zh: “我们认为这是一个新阶段。我们在中国的海外投资中几乎从未真正见过这种情况，”Rhodium Group的高级研究分析师阿曼德·迈耶说。按照他的计算，2024年是中国电动车和电池企业在海外建厂投入高于国内投入的第一年。迈耶继续说：“他们已经准备好走出国内市场，而且他们的竞争力至少与传统西方企业不相上下，甚至更强。”他补充道：“我们认为这仅仅是开始。”

paragraphs.en: Today, some of the world’s best battery research comes from Chinese universities and companies, says Brian Engle, chairman of NAATBatt International, a US trade association for the battery industry. And that’s because China bet on it early.
paragraphs.zh: 美国电池行业贸易协会 NAATBatt 国际的主席布赖恩·恩格尔（Brian Engle）说，如今世界上一些最顶尖的电池研究都来自中国的大学和企业。这是因为中国很早就押注了电池技术。

paragraphs.en: When Engle toured a lab at China’s top engineering school in 2019, he saw more than 60 graduate students building and testing battery cells. Surprised, he turned to an American academic on the tour and asked her how many American universities they’d have to lump together to find as many battery-focused postgrads. “And she said we couldn’t,” he recalled. “We simply couldn’t.”
paragraphs.zh: 2019 年，恩格尔在中国顶尖工科院校的一间实验室参观时，看到有超过60名研究生在制造和测试电池。令他吃惊的是，他转向一位随行的美国学者，问她要找到同样多的“电池方向”研究生，美国要把多少所大学加在一起才够？“她告诉他，他们不可能，”恩格尔回忆道，“我们就是不可能。”

paragraphs.en: So it’s perhaps no surprise that Chinese battery companies are dominant—and that the competition between them is fierce. Nowadays, local incentives and lower shipping costs make it such that opening a factory overseas can be more profitable than staying home. CATL, the world’s largest lithium battery maker, reported in a recent financial filing that its profit margin is 29 percent overseas versus nearly 23 percent in China. Other Chinese companies, including Gotion and EVE Energy, also have reported higher profit margins overseas.
paragraphs.zh: 因此，中国电池企业占据主导地位并展开激烈竞争也就不足为奇了。如今，地方激励和更低的运输成本，使得在海外开厂比在国内经营更赚钱。全球最大锂电池生产商 CATL 最近在一份财报中披露，其海外利润率为 29%，在中国约为 23%。包括国轩高科（Gotion）和亿纬锂能（EVE Energy）在内的其他中国企业也都报告了更高的海外利润率。

paragraphs.en: Macron isn’t the only politician to herald a Chinese battery plant’s arrival. The lovefest is virtually global: Brazil’s Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva rode in a BYD vehicle with the company’s founder. Spain’s prime minister held hands with CATL’s CEO. The governor of Illinois, JB Pritzker, shared a stage with Gotion’s chairman to announce a factory in Manteno, Illinois.
paragraphs.zh: 马可龙（Macron）并不是唯一为中国电池工厂“到来”喝彩的政治人物。对中国电池企业的欢迎几乎是全球性的：巴西总统卢拉（Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva）曾与比亚迪（BYD）创始人同乘车辆；西班牙首相与 CATL 的首席执行官并肩亮相；伊利诺伊州州长 JB·普里茨克尔（JB Pritzker）与国轩高科（Gotion）董事长同台，宣布将在伊利诺伊州曼蒂诺（Manteno）建设工厂。

paragraphs.en: But problems emerge as blueprints turn into massive plants. Factory projects often include promises to hire locally, but sometimes companies bring in migrant labor. In Hungary, local media reported in July that CATL laid off more than 100 employees at a factory, most of them Hungarians, prompting the municipality to launch an investigation and raid the plant. CATL is also facing protests and a lawsuit in Hungary for its water use and environmental footprint—issues commonly faced by battery factories worldwide.
paragraphs.zh: 但当蓝图变成巨型工厂时问题就会显现。工厂项目往往承诺优先雇佣当地人，但有时公司会引进外来劳工。匈牙利媒体在七月报道称，CATL 在一家工厂裁员超过100名员工，其中大多数是匈牙利人，这促使地方政府启动调查并对该工厂进行了突击检查。CATL 也在匈牙利因其用水量和环境足迹问题遭遇抗议和诉讼——这些都是全球电池工厂普遍面临的问题。

paragraphs.en: The situation might sound oddly familiar. When Apple built its technology empire on the backs of Chinese factories, the country had to reckon with whether it was benefiting from Apple’s victories or being exploited. As China’s battery technology takes over the world, Chinese companies are the ones now raising these questions—of who ultimately benefits and who is exploiting whom.
paragraphs.zh: 这种情况听起来似乎奇怪地熟悉。苹果在中国工厂的支撑下建立起其科技帝国时，中国不得不思考自己究竟在享受苹果的成功，还是正在被利用。随着中国的电池技术席卷全球，提出这类问题的现在是中国企业——也就是是谁最终得益，以及谁在剥削谁。
